task: 'when am refreshing studio, this page loads for few seconds' -- a screenshot showed the raw pre-Context-Panel tab bar plus the dead 'Vihu Buddy Coming Soon' placeholder flashing on refresh. Root cause: none of index.html's ~33 script tags are defer/async, so the whole boot sequence (including ContextPanel.init(), which hides this legacy chrome) only runs after DOMContentLoaded -- the raw static markup (.tabs, #story-tab's .buddy-placeholder, visible by default) is genuinely painted for however long those scripts take to load first. Fix: since Context Panel is the permanent architecture with no real fallback, .tabs and .buddy-placeholder are hidden by DEFAULT in base CSS instead of only via the JS-added .context-panel-mode class -- zero value before JS runs, always hidden after, so this removes the flash with no script-loading/architecture change. Separately, 'rename stickers and decoration to just emojis' -- the combined Add Something card ('Stickers & Decor') is now plain 'Emojis', with the matching legacy tab-bar label updated too. A separate report, 'any changes am doing in objects are not reflecting on slides', was investigated via a dedicated Playwright reproduction (Fill Colour edit, Size slider, canvas drag, Text Words edit, all immediately after using the new Back button) and NOT reproduced -- every edit path correctly updates the model and repaints the canvas in the current code; most likely a stale browser cache around the just-shipped build, flagged to the user to hard-refresh and re-report with specifics if it persists. Verified via four_fixes_test.js/add_something_combined_test.js (updated for the Emojis label) plus both goldenBuild.js suites, all passing unchanged, zero console errors. No Theme Contract/Engine V2 Scene Model/Builder change.
